Prefer first-party docs pages such as developers.squarespace.com and support.squarespace.com. Return a concise structured summary with exact source URLs actually visited and the supporting text for each field. Avoid third-party sites and avoid search snippets.

raw_result.description: Website-building and commerce platform with developer tools and commerce APIs for custom integrations and extensions.
raw_result.auth_notes: Squarespace documents two commerce API auth methods: API keys for custom merchant-built applications and OAuth 2.0 for Squarespace Extensions (commercial third-party integrations).
raw_result.gating_reason: API-key access for custom apps is available to merchants on the Commerce Advanced plan; OAuth-based Extensions are available for use by any Squarespace customer, but the documented first toolkit path for direct merchant API-key use requires a paid plan.

api_surface.breadth: Public commerce REST APIs include Contacts, Inventory, Orders, Products, Transactions, Analytics, and Webhook Subscriptions.
api_surface.mcp_notes: No first-party Squarespace MCP/server offering was found in the official developer documentation reviewed.

raw_result.blocker: Direct API-key integrations require a Commerce Advanced paid plan; OAuth is aimed at Extensions, so first-toolkit setup may depend on plan choice and integration model.
raw_result.rate_limit_notes: Squarespace documents a standard limit of 300 requests per minute (about 5 requests/second average) and a stricter 100 requests/hour limit for the Create Order endpoint when using an API key.
raw_result.notable_signals: Official docs clearly distinguish merchant custom apps from commercial Extensions, and publish concrete endpoint families plus rate limits.
